cases.title: Scenario-matrix coverage for rotating QR token in open session
preconditions: Lecturer is authenticated and assigned to the class section.
Class session is in Open state.
steps: Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current and capture qrPayload and expiresAt.
Wait until after first expiresAt and call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current again.
Compare token metadata between the two responses.
cases.expected: QR token metadata aligns with testing-plan QR row: each token is valid for a 30-second window and next retrieval after expiry returns a new valid token for the same open session.

cases.title: QR issuance module persists 30-second TTL token lifecycle at DB boundary
preconditions: Integration suite runs against real database transaction boundaries.
An Open class session exists with lecturer assignment.
steps: Invoke QR issuance/read module for the open session and persist token metadata.
Assert stored token expiry is exactly issuance time plus 30 seconds.
Advance time beyond TTL and invoke issuance/read again.
Assert newly persisted token differs from previous token and is marked valid for the new 30-second window.
cases.expected: Service + repository integration enforces deterministic 30-second TTL and rotation persistence without stale token reuse after expiry.

cases.title: Token validity follows session state transitions open and close
preconditions: Scheduled session exists for assigned lecturer.
Session control and QR services are wired to same database.
steps: Execute POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/open as assigned Lecturer.
Verify QR token can be issued while state is Open.
Execute POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close.
Attempt to use previously issued token in check-in flow after close.
cases.expected: Legal Scheduled->Open transition enables QR issuance; after Open->Closed transition, prior token is no longer accepted for successful check-in and no illegal token-valid state persists.

cases.title: Parallel check-ins with same valid token keep independent outcomes
preconditions: Session is Open and one QR token is currently valid.
At least two enrolled students are prepared for concurrent submissions.
Integration harness can dispatch parallel POST /v1/check-ins requests.
steps: Send concurrent POST /v1/check-ins using the same qrToken for two different enrolled students before expiry.
For one of those students, immediately send a second concurrent retry for the same session.
Inspect attendance records and check-in-attempt rows.
cases.expected: Token is multi-use within TTL across different students, while duplicate success is prevented per student-session: each student has at most one success and duplicate attempts return DuplicateCheckIn.

cases.title: HTTP contract for current QR endpoint exposes valid envelope and expiry fields
preconditions: Assigned Lecturer is authenticated.
Target session is currently Open.
steps: Send GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current with Authorization header.
Validate HTTP status and response envelope keys data/meta/error.
Validate data includes classSessionId, tokenState=Valid, expiresAt, and qrPayload.
Re-call endpoint after TTL rollover and confirm response remains contract-compliant with refreshed token metadata.
cases.expected: Endpoint returns 200 and stable envelope schema; qr/current data remains contract-valid across token rollover with updated expiry metadata.

cases.title: Primary lifecycle flow open session then student checks in with active token
preconditions: Lecturer account and enrolled student account are available.
Session starts in Scheduled state.
steps: Lecturer opens attendance via POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/open.
Student submits POST /v1/check-ins with qrToken from active display before expiry.
Lecturer requests current QR metadata while session remains open.
cases.expected: Main flow succeeds end-to-end: session opens, student check-in accepts within TTL window, and QR remains actively refreshed for continued use without requiring session reopen.

cases.title: Alternate flow expired token fails then refreshed token succeeds
preconditions: Open session with rotating QR token is available.
Student is authenticated and enrolled.
steps: Capture a QR token and wait until after its expiresAt boundary.
Submit POST /v1/check-ins with the expired token.
Fetch current token from active QR display endpoint and submit POST /v1/check-ins again within TTL.
cases.expected: First attempt fails due to expiry, while follow-up attempt with newly rotated valid token can succeed in the same open session.

cases.title: Unauthorized and out-of-scope actors are denied report and export APIs
preconditions: Student account and Lecturer account outside target section scope exist.
Attendance data exists in restricted section scope.
steps: Call GET /v1/reports/attendance as Student and out-of-scope Lecturer.
Call POST /v1/exports/attendance using filters outside each actor scope.
Inspect responses for authorization behavior and data leakage.
cases.expected: Denied actors receive authorization denial semantics and cannot retrieve restricted report rows, pagination counts, or export jobs outside permitted scope.

cases.title: TTL boundary enforcement returns structured ExpiredQr error
preconditions: Student is authenticated and enrolled in an Open session.
A token with known expiresAt is available.
steps: Submit POST /v1/check-ins just after token expiresAt.
Record HTTP status and error.code payload.
Verify failed attempt is queryable in check-in attempt logs.
cases.expected: API rejects out-of-window token with documented structured error behavior (ExpiredQr), and failure is persisted for audit/attempt coverage without creating successful attendance.

cases.title: Lecturer UI journey shows auto-rotating QR without manual page reload
preconditions: Lecturer is logged in on desktop browser and has access to an Open session page with QR panel.
Session row and QR panel are visible in UI.
steps: Open the live attendance session page and observe current QR token and countdown/expiry indicator.
Wait past one TTL cycle without refreshing browser tab.
Confirm QR display updates to a new token and remains valid for ongoing student scans.
Verify session state indicator remains Open during token rotations.
cases.expected: Browser flow demonstrates UI auto-refresh of QR token every TTL cycle without lecturer reload, with consistent open-state indicators and usable next-token continuity.
